tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.description: General-purpose AI assistant. Free and Pro tiers widely used by individuals without IT knowledge or approval.
commonUseCases: Writing job descriptions and HR policies

regulatoryFlags.description: Data processor obligations — OpenAI acting as processor without a formal DPA in place for free/Pro tier users
International data transfers — data processed on US servers without adequate transfer mechanisms for free tier
Deployer obligations when using general-purpose AI in professional context
Same DPA and transfer obligations apply post-Brexit under UK equivalent

tools.enterpriseMitigation: OpenAI Enterprise plan provides DPA, data is not used for training, SOC2 compliance. Free/Pro tiers do not provide these guarantees.
mitigations: Procure OpenAI Enterprise with signed DPA
Publish acceptable use policy prohibiting personal data input on free/Pro tier
Staff training on what constitutes personal or sensitive data
Technical controls to detect ChatGPT access on corporate devices
tools.notes: The most widely deployed shadow AI tool globally. Assume it is in use across all departments unless actively controlled.

tools.name: Microsoft Copilot (Consumer / Web)
tools.description: Consumer-facing Copilot accessible via browser and integrated into Windows. Distinct from Microsoft 365 Copilot enterprise product.

regulatoryFlags.description: Consumer tier does not provide enterprise DPA — data handling terms apply for personal accounts
General-purpose AI model obligations apply

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Microsoft 365 Copilot (enterprise) provides DPA and does not use customer data for training. Ensure staff are using enterprise, not consumer, version.
mitigations: Audit whether staff are using consumer or enterprise Copilot
Disable consumer Copilot on managed devices via Intune/Group Policy
Ensure M365 Copilot enterprise licence is in place before encouraging use
tools.notes: Particularly insidious because it appears legitimate — it is a Microsoft product — but the consumer version has very different data handling terms to the enterprise product.

tools.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot (Enterprise)
tools.description: Enterprise AI assistant integrated into Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams. Licenced separately.
dataTypesAtRisk: all internal data accessible to the user's M365 account

regulatoryFlags.description: Compliant under enterprise DPA — but over-permissioned users may expose data they should not have access to
Deployer obligations apply — organisation must assess use cases, particularly in HR and performance management contexts

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Enterprise product with DPA. Primary risk is internal — Copilot surfaces data the user has access to, so over-permissioned users can inadvertently expose sensitive data.
mitigations: Review and tighten M365 permissions before enabling Copilot
Audit SharePoint and OneDrive access controls
Define approved and prohibited Copilot use cases
Train staff on what Copilot can and cannot access
tools.notes: Often rolled out without accompanying permission review. The AI does not create data access problems — it amplifies existing ones.

tools.name: Google Gemini (Consumer)
tools.description: Google's AI assistant. Free tier widely accessible. Integrated into Google Search, Chrome, and Android.

regulatoryFlags.description: Free tier terms allow Google to use inputs to improve models — no DPA for personal use
Data processed in US — transfer mechanism requirements apply
General-purpose AI model obligations

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Google Workspace with Gemini for Google Workspace addon provides enterprise data protection. Free consumer Gemini does not.
mitigations: Block consumer Gemini on corporate devices
Procure Gemini for Google Workspace if using Google Workspace
Explicit policy on use of Google consumer AI tools
tools.notes: Increasingly embedded in Android devices and Chrome browser, making it accessible without deliberate installation.

tools.name: Grammarly
tools.description: AI-powered writing assistant. Browser extension installs silently and intercepts all text input across all web applications.
dataTypesAtRisk: all typed text — including passwords, PII, confidential documents, client data, financial data

regulatoryFlags.description: Grammarly processes all text typed in the browser as a data processor — requires DPA for enterprise use
Data minimisation — Grammarly free tier collects more data than necessary for the service

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Grammarly Business provides DPA and enhanced data controls. Free personal accounts do not.
mitigations: Audit browser extensions across managed devices — Grammarly is often installed without IT knowledge
Block Grammarly personal accounts via endpoint management
Procure Grammarly Business with DPA if the tool is needed
Include browser extensions in acceptable use policy
tools.notes: One of the highest-risk shadow AI tools because it is invisible — employees do not consider it an 'AI tool' and IT teams frequently overlook it. Intercepts every keystroke in the browser.

tools.name: Notion AI
tools.description: AI features built into Notion workspace. Often adopted by teams as an unofficial knowledge base before IT is aware.

regulatoryFlags.description: DPA available but may not be in place for teams that adopted Notion informally

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Notion Enterprise provides DPA, SSO, and admin controls. Team or personal plans lack adequate enterprise data controls.
mitigations: Audit for unofficial Notion workspaces in use across teams
Centralise onto enterprise plan with SSO
Data classification policy for what can be stored in Notion
tools.notes: Shadow IT risk predates the AI features — teams adopt Notion independently. AI features add a new data processing layer to an already ungoverned tool.

tools.name: Claude (Consumer / claude.ai)
tools.description: Anthropic's AI assistant. Free and Pro tiers. Strong reasoning capability drives professional adoption.

regulatoryFlags.description: Free/Pro tier does not provide enterprise DPA — data handling governed by consumer terms
Data processed on US infrastructure

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Anthropic Claude for Enterprise provides DPA, zero data retention, and admin controls.
mitigations: Acceptable use policy covering claude.ai consumer use
Procure Claude for Enterprise if significant use is detected
Training on what constitutes sensitive data
tools.notes: Particularly likely to be used by high-value staff (legal, finance, strategy) for sophisticated tasks — the very use cases where data sensitivity is highest.

tools.name: Perplexity AI
tools.description: AI-powered search and research tool. Growing rapidly as a Google alternative among knowledge workers.
commonUseCases: Background checks on companies and people
dataTypesAtRisk: research queries may reveal strategic intent, M&A activity, competitive intelligence

regulatoryFlags.description: Queries processed on US infrastructure — query content may reveal confidential strategy

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Lower direct data risk than generative tools — primary risk is query content revealing strategic intent rather than data being uploaded.
mitigations: Include in AI tool policy
Train staff on query hygiene — do not include company names, client names, or confidential context in searches
tools.notes: Lower risk than generative tools but search queries can reveal strategic intent (e.g. researching a competitor, an M&A target, or a regulatory issue).

tools.name: Midjourney
tools.description: AI image generation tool accessed via Discord. Free tier generates images in public Discord channels by default.

regulatoryFlags.description: Ownership of AI-generated images is legally unclear in many jurisdictions — commercial use risk
If prompts include descriptions of real people, GDPR obligations may apply

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Midjourney Pro plan provides private mode (images not shown in public channels). Free tier exposes all prompts and outputs publicly.
mitigations: Prohibit free tier use for any work-related content
Procure Pro plan with private mode if image generation is needed
IP policy covering AI-generated content ownership
tools.notes: Free tier is particularly dangerous — all prompts and images are visible in public Discord channels, meaning unreleased campaign concepts or product ideas are publicly visible.

tools.name: ElevenLabs
tools.description: AI voice cloning and text-to-speech. Capable of cloning a voice from a short audio sample.
dataTypesAtRisk: voice biometrics of executives or staff

regulatoryFlags.description: Voice data may constitute biometric data — special category data requiring explicit consent
Biometric identification systems subject to high-risk classification requirements

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Explicit consent required before cloning any voice. Enterprise plan provides data controls.
mitigations: Explicit policy prohibiting voice cloning of any person without written consent
Include in AI acceptable use policy
Assess biometric data classification under GDPR Article 9
tools.notes: Low adoption likelihood but critical risk if used — voice cloning of executives creates fraud and reputational risk. Also a social engineering vector.

tools.name: Runway
tools.description: AI video generation and editing. Can generate video from text prompts or edit existing footage.

regulatoryFlags.description: Video containing identifiable individuals requires lawful basis for processing
Training data and output ownership legally unclear

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Enterprise plan provides data controls and clearer IP terms.
mitigations: Policy on AI video generation covering IP and GDPR
Prohibit uploading footage of identifiable individuals without consent
tools.notes: Emerging risk as AI video becomes mainstream. Watch for rapid adoption in marketing and communications teams.

tools.name: GitHub Copilot
tools.description: AI code completion integrated into VS Code and other IDEs. Personal accounts may be used without IT approval.
dataTypesAtRisk: API keys and secrets in code

regulatoryFlags.description: Copilot trained on public code — output may reproduce GPL-licensed code without attribution
If code contains personal data (e.g. hardcoded test data), this is processed by GitHub servers

tools.enterpriseMitigation: GitHub Copilot Business/Enterprise provides DPA, code is not used for training, and admin controls over which repositories it can access.
mitigations: Audit developer personal Copilot subscriptions
Centralise onto GitHub Copilot Business with DPA
Policy prohibiting hardcoded secrets or personal data in code
Secret scanning in CI/CD pipeline
tools.notes: Developers frequently subscribe personally before IT has a policy. Personal accounts mean code snippets may be used to train future models.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.description: AI-native code editor built on VS Code. Sends code to external AI models for analysis and generation.

regulatoryFlags.description: Entire codebase sent to external servers — significant IP exposure risk
Any personal data in the codebase is processed externally

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Cursor Business plan provides data controls. Privacy mode prevents code from being used for training.
mitigations: Enable privacy mode at minimum
Procure Business plan with DPA
Policy on what codebases can be opened in AI-native editors
Never open production infrastructure code in personal AI code editors
tools.notes: Higher risk than GitHub Copilot because it indexes and sends larger code context. The convenience is significantly higher — so adoption is rapid once discovered.

tools.name: Zapier / Make (with AI steps)
tools.description: Automation platforms that now include AI steps. Staff build automations that route business data through external AI models.

regulatoryFlags.description: Automations routing personal data require DPA with each processor in the chain
Records of processing obligations — informal automations are rarely documented

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Enterprise plans for both platforms provide DPA. The governance challenge is visibility — informal automations built by individuals are difficult to discover.
mitigations: Audit for personal Zapier/Make accounts in use across the business
Centralise automation platforms under IT governance
Require all automations to be documented in ROPA (Records of Processing Activities)
Policy prohibiting personal data in unapproved automation workflows
tools.notes: The silent multiplier — one employee building a Zapier automation can route thousands of customer records through an ungoverned AI pipeline without IT ever knowing.

tools.name: Otter.ai
tools.description: AI meeting transcription and summarisation. Often installed by individuals to record and transcribe meetings.
likelyDepts: all — particularly sales, hr, executive, legal

regulatoryFlags.description: Recording individuals requires lawful basis — participants must be informed and consent obtained where required
Transcripts of HR or health-related meetings may contain special category data
Transcripts of legally privileged discussions stored on third-party servers may waive privilege

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Otter.ai Business provides DPA. The consent and notification obligation applies regardless of plan tier.
mitigations: Policy requiring disclosure when any recording or transcription tool is active in a meeting
Prohibit use in legally privileged, HR, or board discussions
DPA with Otter.ai if used on enterprise plan
Train staff on consent obligations before recording
tools.notes: Extremely high risk in regulated industries. Legal and HR conversations are the most common use case — also the highest sensitivity. Recording without consent is a GDPR violation and potential criminal offence in some jurisdictions.

tools.name: Beautiful.ai / Gamma
tools.description: AI-powered presentation builders. Staff paste content — including confidential strategy, financials, and client data — to auto-generate slides.

regulatoryFlags.description: Presentation content including personal data requires DPA
Strategy and financial data pasted into consumer tools may be used for model training

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Enterprise plans provide DPA and data controls. Consumer tiers vary — check terms of service.
mitigations: Policy on pasting confidential content into AI presentation tools
Procure enterprise plan with DPA for approved use
Data classification training — staff must recognise what is and isn't suitable for external AI tools
tools.notes: Presentation tools feel low-risk because the output is visual — staff do not perceive pasting strategy documents as a data exposure event.

tools.name: Adobe Firefly / Adobe AI
tools.description: AI image generation and editing integrated into Adobe Creative Cloud. Enterprise customers may assume compliance without verifying terms.

regulatoryFlags.description: Adobe states Firefly outputs are commercially safe — but terms should be verified for enterprise use

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Adobe Creative Cloud Enterprise provides clearer IP indemnification. Enterprise customers have stronger data controls.
mitigations: Verify enterprise vs personal Creative Cloud licences
Review IP indemnification terms for commercial output
Policy on AI-generated assets in client deliverables
tools.notes: Lower risk than many tools due to Adobe's enterprise focus and IP indemnification commitments, but terms should be reviewed rather than assumed.

tools.name: Harvey AI
tools.description: AI platform specifically designed for legal work. Used by law firms and legal teams for contract analysis, drafting, and research.

regulatoryFlags.description: Uploading privileged documents to any third-party AI platform requires careful privilege analysis
DPA required — Harvey provides enterprise DPA but verification is needed
Solicitors must maintain client confidentiality — use of external AI tools must be disclosed to clients in some jurisdictions

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Harvey is built for enterprise legal use with appropriate data controls. Risk is primarily privilege and professional conduct, not data security.
mitigations: Legal privilege analysis before uploading any privileged materials
Client disclosure policy for AI tool use
SRA / Bar guidance review on AI tool obligations
tools.notes: Lower shadow IT risk (expensive, specialist tool) but extremely high legal and professional conduct risk when used. Relevant primarily to legal sector clients.

tools.name: Slack AI
tools.description: AI features built into Slack that summarise channels, threads, and messages. Surfaces content the user may not have intended to share — including messages from private channels if permissions allow.

regulatoryFlags.description: Slack AI processes workspace content; DPA required and must cover AI features specifically
Deployer obligations apply when using AI to process employee communications

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Slack AI is only available on paid plans with enterprise data controls. Ensure DPA covers AI features. Audit which channels AI can access — private channels may be included.
mitigations: Verify DPA covers Slack AI specifically — standard Slack DPA may predate AI features
Audit channel permissions before enabling Slack AI
Policy on what topics may be discussed in Slack given AI summarisation
Consider disabling Slack AI in channels containing HR or legally sensitive discussions
tools.notes: High adoption risk because Slack AI activates within an existing approved tool — staff and IT teams may not notice it has been enabled. Channel history going back years is suddenly being processed by AI.

tools.name: Zoom AI Companion
tools.description: AI meeting summarisation, transcription, and Q&A built into Zoom. Automatically generates meeting summaries shared with participants and potentially stored in Zoom's cloud.
likelyDepts: all — wherever Zoom is used for meetings

regulatoryFlags.description: Recording and processing meeting content requires lawful basis; participants must be informed
Transcripts of health or HR meetings may contain special category data
Transcripts of legally privileged discussions stored on Zoom servers may waive privilege
Automated processing of employee communications triggers deployer obligations

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Zoom AI Companion is included in paid Zoom plans. Enterprise customers can configure data retention and residency. Consent and notification obligations apply regardless of plan.
mitigations: Enable notification to all participants when AI Companion is active
Prohibit AI Companion in legally privileged, HR, and board meetings
Configure Zoom data retention policy for AI-generated transcripts
Review and update meeting recording consent policies to cover AI summarisation
Audit who receives AI-generated summaries — default may be broader than intended
tools.notes: Extremely high risk in regulated industries. Unlike Otter.ai which requires deliberate installation, Zoom AI Companion is built in and can be activated by any meeting host. Participants may not know their words are being transcribed and summarised.

tools.name: Apple Intelligence
tools.description: AI features built into iOS 18+, macOS Sequoia+, and iPadOS. Processes emails, messages, documents, and notifications on-device and via Apple's Private Cloud Compute. Some features route through OpenAI (ChatGPT integration).
likelyDepts: all — on personal and managed Apple devices
dataTypesAtRisk: any content visible to the OS

regulatoryFlags.description: Where content is processed via Private Cloud Compute or routed to OpenAI, data processor obligations apply
Data processed on US infrastructure when routed to Apple or OpenAI servers
General-purpose AI obligations apply to ChatGPT integration

tools.enterpriseMitigation: Apple Business Manager allows MDM policies to restrict or disable Apple Intelligence features on managed devices. The ChatGPT integration can be disabled separately from on-device features.
mitigations: Audit MDM policy — ensure Apple Intelligence is explicitly addressed
Disable ChatGPT integration via MDM on managed devices
Update BYOD policy to address Apple Intelligence on personal devices used for work
Staff awareness that AI features may process work emails and documents on personal phones
tools.notes: Particularly insidious on BYOD devices — work emails and documents visible on a personal iPhone are being processed by Apple Intelligence without any organisational control. The ChatGPT integration is off by default but easily enabled by users.

tools.name: WhatsApp / Meta AI
tools.description: Meta AI is embedded directly into WhatsApp and Instagram. Staff using WhatsApp for work communications may inadvertently use AI features.
likelyDepts: all — where WhatsApp is used for business

regulatoryFlags.description: Meta's data processing terms for consumer WhatsApp are not appropriate for business personal data
Data minimisation — WhatsApp collects extensive metadata even without AI features

tools.enterpriseMitigation: WhatsApp Business API with enterprise data processing agreement is appropriate for business use. Consumer WhatsApp for business communications is a pre-existing governance issue that AI features exacerbate.
mitigations: Policy on use of consumer messaging apps for business communications
Migrate to approved business communication platform
Explicit guidance on Meta AI features in WhatsApp
This is often a pre-existing governance issue — AI features make it more urgent to resolve
tools.notes: The AI risk is secondary to the underlying governance issue of consumer apps being used for business. AI features turn a communication governance problem into a data processing one.

financial-services.specificConcerns: FCA's PS7/24 on AI governance, FCA operational resilience requirements, SMCR accountability for AI decisions
financial-services.notes: Financial services has the most mature regulatory framework for AI. FCA expects firms to demonstrate AI governance. SR 11-7 equivalent expectations are emerging.

legal.specificConcerns: Legal professional privilege, client confidentiality obligations, SRA guidance on AI tool use, conflict checking
legal.notes: Legal privilege is the primary differentiator for legal sector risk — once privileged documents are shared with a third-party AI, privilege may be waived.

healthcare.specificConcerns: Special category health data, clinical AI high-risk classification under EU AI Act, NHS data residency requirements
healthcare.notes: Health data is special category under GDPR. EU AI Act classifies AI systems used in healthcare decisions as high-risk, triggering conformity assessment requirements.

professional-services.specificConcerns: Client confidentiality, IP ownership of AI-assisted deliverables, staff productivity vs risk trade-off
professional-services.notes: Professional services firms face high pressure to adopt AI for productivity while managing client confidentiality obligations. The conflict between speed and governance is acute.

public-sector.specificConcerns: Official Sensitive data classification, Cabinet Office AI guidance, procurement rules for AI tools, democratic accountability
public-sector.notes: Public sector AI use is subject to increasing scrutiny. Government AI Framework requires transparency. Some data classifications (Official Sensitive and above) cannot be processed on commercial AI platforms.

retail-ecommerce.specificConcerns: Customer PII in AI workflows, AI-generated marketing compliance, personalisation transparency
retail-ecommerce.notes: Retail sector often has large volumes of customer PII flowing through marketing and operations — AI tools in these workflows create significant GDPR exposure.

keyPrinciples: Never input personal data, client data, or confidential company information into any AI tool without IT approval
All AI tools must be approved before use — personal accounts are not permitted for work purposes
AI-generated output must be reviewed and verified by a human before use in client-facing or business-critical contexts
Any data breach or suspected data exposure via an AI tool must be reported immediately
Use of AI tools must be disclosed where required by professional regulations or client agreements

levels.examples: Published content, press releases, public datasets

levels.aiUse: Approved enterprise AI tools with DPA only
Approved enterprise AI tools with DPA and data residency controls
levels.examples: Client data, financial data, strategy documents, contracts

levels.aiUse: On-premises or air-gapped AI only — no external processing
levels.examples: Legally privileged, M&A sensitive, health data, Official Sensitive
